items.name: 211 Ontario
items.access_script: Call 211. No fees.

items.name: 211 Ontario
items.access_script: Call 211 for assistance, or visit https://211ontario.ca. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: 9-8-8 Suicide Crisis Helpline
items.name_fr: 9-8-8 Ligne d'aide en cas de crise de suicide
items.access_script: Call or text 988. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Aboriginal Legal Services (ALS)
items.access_script: Indigenous people in Ontario can call 1-844-633-2886 to reach Aboriginal Legal Services for free legal advice and representation. No fees are charged for their assistance.

items.name: ACFOMI
items.access_script: Call 613-546-7863 for assistance, or visit https://acfomi.ca.

items.name: ACFOMI (Association canadienne-française de l'Ontario)
items.access_script: Hi, I am looking for help finding work in Kingston. Do you offer services in French?

items.name: Advocacy Centre for the Elderly (ACE)
items.name_fr: Centre de défense des personnes âgées (ACE)
items.access_script: Ontario seniors (age 60 and over) can call 1-855-598-2656 for free legal advice on elder law issues (e.g., long-term care, elder abuse, health care consent).

items.name: Alzheimer Society KFL&A
items.access_script: Visit https://alzheimer.ca/kfla for information.

items.name: AMHS-KFLA 24/7 Crisis Line
items.access_script: Hi, I'm feeling really overwhelmed and having suicidal thoughts. I need to talk to someone right away.

items.name: AMS Food Bank (Queen's)
items.access_script: Call 613-533-6000 for assistance, or visit https://www.amsfoodbank.ca.

items.name: ARCH Disability Law Centre
items.access_script: Call 1-866-482-2724 or email archlib@lao.on.ca. No fees.

items.name: Arthritis Society Canada
items.access_script: Call 1-800-321-1433 to speak with a trained volunteer about arthritis-related questions. This free support line is available to anyone living with arthritis or caring for someone who is.

items.name: Artillery Park Aquatic Centre
items.access_script: Visit 382 Bagot St, Kingston, ON K7K 3B6. Fees: Varies (drop-in or membership).

items.name: Assaulted Women's Helpline (AWHL)
items.access_script: Hello, I am in an unsafe situation at home and I need to know my options for leaving.

items.name: Assaulted Women's Helpline (AWHL)
items.access_script: Call 1-866-863-0511. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Autism Ontario (East Region)
items.access_script: Visit https://www.autismontario.com for information.

items.name: BGC South East
items.access_script: Hi, I'd like to register my child for the after-school program. Is there space available?

items.name: BGC South East (Boys & Girls Club)
items.access_script: Visit https://www.bgcsoutheast.ca for information.

items.name: Black Youth Helpline
items.access_script: Call 1-833-294-8650 for assistance, or visit https://blackyouth.ca. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Boots on the Ground
items.access_script: Ontario first responders (police, fire, EMS, corrections, etc.) can call 1-833-677-2668 for anonymous peer support. This helpline is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: BounceBack Ontario
items.access_script: Ontario residents (age 15+) with a valid OHIP card can join the free BounceBack program. Call 1-866-345-0224 or visit bouncebackontario.ca to learn how to get started (self-referral or provider referral accepted).

items.name: Canadian Red Cross - Kingston
items.access_script: Visit https://www.redcross.ca for information.

items.name: Cancer Information Helpline
items.access_script: Call 1-888-939-3333 for information on cancer types, treatments, side effects, and local support services. This free helpline is open to patients, caregivers, and anyone with cancer-related questions.

items.name: CDK Family Medicine and Walk-In Clinic
items.name_fr: Clinique sans rendez-vous de médecine familiale CDK
items.access_script: Hi, I have a bad cough and fever and need to see a doctor today. Are you accepting walk-ins?

items.name: City of Kingston - Housing and Social Services (Ontario Works)
items.access_script: Hi, I'm currently unemployed and have no income. How do I apply for Ontario Works?

items.name: City of Kingston - Housing and Social Services (Ontario Works)
items.name_fr: Ville de Kingston - Logement et services sociaux
items.access_script: Call 613-546-2695 for assistance, or visit https://www.cityofkingston.ca.

items.name: CLEO (Community Legal Education Ontario)
items.access_script: Visit https://www.cleo.on.ca. No fees.

items.name: CNIB Foundation Kingston
items.access_script: Visit https://cnib.ca for information.

items.name: COAST (Crisis Outreach)
items.access_script: Call 613-544-4229 for assistance, or visit https://amhs-kfla.ca. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Community Foundation for Kingston & Area
items.name_fr: Fondation communautaire de Kingston et de la région
items.access_script: Visit https://cfka.org for information.

items.name: Community Harvest Market (Rideau Heights)
items.access_script: Visit https://www.lovingspoonful.org/programs/community-harvest-markets/ for dates and locations. For current-season confirmation, contact Kingston Community Health Centre (Rideau Heights) at 613-542-2949 or info@kchc.ca.

items.name: Community Living Kingston and District
items.access_script: Hi, I'm looking for support services for my adult child with an intellectual disability. Who can I speak to?

items.name: ConnexOntario
items.access_script: Call 1-866-531-2600 or visit https://connexontario.ca. No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Consumer Protection Ontario
items.access_script: Ontario consumers can call 1-800-889-9768 for free information about consumer rights or to report unfair business practices. This is the provincial Consumer Protection Ontario helpline.

items.name: Crisis Text Line
items.access_script: Call 686868 (SMS). No fees. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Dawn House
items.access_script: Hi, I'm looking for long-term housing support for women. How can I get on your waitlist?

items.name: Dawn House Services and Housing for Women
items.name_fr: Services et logement pour femmes Dawn House
items.access_script: Call 613-545-1379 or 613-929-3440 or email info@dawnhouse.ca. Intake assessment required. Fees: Shelter is free. Housing is rent-geared-to-income. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

items.name: Diabetes Canada Information & Support
items.access_script: Call 1-800-226-8464 for free information and support related to diabetes management and resources. This service is available to anyone affected by diabetes.

items.name: EarlyON Child and Family Centre
items.name_fr: Centre pour l'enfant et la famille ON y va
items.access_script: Visit https://kchc.ca/weller-avenue/earlyon/ for information.

items.name: EarlyON Child and Family Centre (KCHC)
items.name_fr: Centre pour l'enfant et la famille ON y va (KCHC)
items.access_script: Hi, I have a 2-year-old and I'm looking for a playgroup. Do I need to register for the drop-in?
